'''屋主忍男武雄心命''(やぬしおしおたけおごころのみこと;生没年不詳)は[[3世紀]]から[[4世紀]]頃の日本の[[皇族]]。[[彦太忍信命]](ひこふつおしのまことのみこと)の王子で第8代[[孝元天皇]]の孫に当たる。[[莵道彦]](うじひこ/宇豆比古)の娘[[影媛]](かげひめ)を妃とし[[武内宿禰]]を儲ける。[[古事記]]で武内宿禰は彦太忍信命の王子となっており、屋主忍男武雄心命は登場しない。
[[日本書紀]]景行紀に[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日に景行天皇が紀の国に行幸し神祇を祀ろうとしたが占いによると吉と出なかった為、命が天皇より紀の国に派遣され阿備の柏原で神祇を祀り同地に定住して紀直の後裔莵道彦の娘影媛を娶り武内宿禰が生まれたとある。
命は[[井石神社]]([[長崎県]][[東彼杵郡]][[波佐見町]])・[[千栗八幡宮]]([[佐賀県]][[三養基郡]][[みやき町]])・[[武雄神社]]([[佐賀県]][[武雄市]])・[[江野神社]]([[新潟県]][[上越市]])・[[甲良神社]]([[滋賀県]][[犬上郡]][[甲良町]])・[[五社神社]]([[滋賀県]][[蒲生郡]][[日野町]])・[[馬見岡綿向神社]]([[滋賀県]][[蒲生郡]][[日野町]])・[[若櫻神社]][[摂社]]高屋安倍神社([[奈良県]][[桜井市]])で祀られている。

'''屋主忍男武雄心命'''（やぬしおしおたけおごころのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[3世紀]]から[[4世紀]]頃の[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。[[彦太忍信命]]（ひこふつおしのまことのみこと）の[[王子]]で第8代[[孝元天皇]]の[[親族呼称|孫]]に当たる。[[莵道彦]]（うじひこ／宇豆比古）の[[続柄|娘]][[影媛]]（かげひめ）を妃とし[[武内宿禰]]を儲ける。[[古事記]]で武内宿禰は彦太忍信命の王子となっており、屋主忍男武雄心命は登場しない。

'''屋主忍男武雄心命'''（やぬしおしおたけおごころのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[3世紀]]から[[4世紀]]頃の[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。[[彦太忍信命]]（ひこふつおしのまことのみこと）の[[王子]]で第8代[[孝元天皇]]の[[親族呼称|孫]]に当たる。[[莵道彦]]（うじひこ／宇豆比古）の[[続柄|娘]][[影媛]]（かげひめ）を妃とし[[武内宿禰]]を儲ける。[[古事記]]で武内宿禰は彦太忍信命の王子となっており、屋主忍男武雄心命は登場しない。
[[日本書紀]]景行紀に[[景行天皇]]3年[[2月1日]]に、景行天皇が紀の国に[[行幸]]し、神祇を祀ろうとしたが、[[占い]]によると吉と出なかったため、命が天皇より紀の国に派遣され、阿備の柏原で神祇を祀り、同地に定住して紀直の後裔莵道彦の娘影媛を娶り、武内宿禰が生まれたとある。 また[[高橋氏文]]に、[[磐鹿六雁命]]が卒した際、宣命使として遣わされた「武男心命」が見え、あるいは同一人物か。
命は[[井石神社]]（[[長崎県]][[東彼杵郡]][[波佐見町]]）・[[千栗八幡宮]]（[[佐賀県]][[三養基郡]][[みやき町]]）・[[武雄神社]]（佐賀県[[武雄市]]）・[[江野神社]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]）・[[甲良神社]]（[[滋賀県]][[犬上郡]][[甲良町]]）・[[五社神社 (日野町)|五社神社]]（滋賀県[[蒲生郡]][[日野町 (滋賀県)|日野町]]）・[[馬見岡綿向神社]]（滋賀県蒲生郡日野町)・[[若櫻神社]][[摂社]]高屋安倍神社（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]）などで祀られている。
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やぬしおしおたけおこころのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]]

revision.comment: 全体的に加筆・修正・整理、出典付け。
revision.text:
'''屋主忍男武雄心命'''（やぬしおしおたけおごころのみこと、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。
『[[日本書紀]]』では「屋主忍男武雄心命」「武猪心命」、他文献では「建猪心命」「屋主忍雄命」「武男心命」等とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。
第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇孫で、[[武内宿禰]]の父とされる人物である{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 系譜 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では父祖に関する明記はないが、孝元天皇7年2月2日条において第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇子の[[彦太忍信命]]（ひこふつおいのまことのみこと）を武内宿禰の祖父とするため、屋主忍男武雄心命は彦太忍信命の子にあたる。
同書[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条では子に関して、[[菟道彦]]（[[紀伊国造|紀直]]遠祖）の娘の[[影媛]]（かげひめ）との間に[[武内宿禰]]があるとする{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また[[応神天皇]]9年4月条では、武内宿禰の弟の[[甘美内宿禰]]も子として見える。
一方『[[古事記]]』では、孝元天皇皇子の比古布都押之信命（彦太忍信命）の子が建内宿禰（武内宿禰）になるとしており、屋主忍男武雄心命は系譜から外れている{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
また『紀氏系図』では、孝元天皇皇子に彦太忍信命、その子に屋主忍雄命、その子に武内宿禰と甘美内宿禰の名を記載する{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条によると、天皇は[[紀伊国|紀伊]]に行幸して神祇祭祀を行おうとしたが、占いで不吉と出たため、代わりに武雄心命が遣わされた。武雄心命が阿備柏原（あびのかしわばら：現・[[和歌山市]]相原・柏原か）にて留まり住むこと9年、その間に[[影媛]]との間に[[武内宿禰]]を儲けたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『[[政事要略]]』所引「[[高橋氏文]]」によると、膳氏祖の[[磐鹿六雁命]]が死去した際に、景行天皇によって藤河別命・武男心命（武雄心命）が宣命使として派遣されたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、[[垂仁天皇]]の癸酉年2月1日に武雄心命は住吉大神の願に従って遣わされ、山を寄せて幣とし、阿備柏原社で斎祀し、9年のうちに難破道龍住山（比定地未詳）を申し賜ったという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
== 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 紀朝臣 - 石川朝臣同氏。屋主忍雄建猪心命の後。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=屋主忍男武雄心命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[武内宿禰]] * 屋主忍男武雄心命を祭神とする主な神社<!--式内社等、主な神社に限る--> ** [[江野神社]] （[[新潟県]][[上越市]]） ** [[若櫻神社]][[摂社]]の高屋安倍神社 （[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]）
{{DEFAULTSORT:やぬしおしおたけおこころのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]]

{{Small|（蘇賀石河宿禰）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01=[[平群木菟|平群木菟宿禰]]
{{Small|（平群都久宿禰）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01=[[紀角|紀角宿禰]]
{{Small|（木角宿禰）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|久米能摩伊刀比売}}
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|怒能伊呂比売}}

{{Small|（葛城長江曾都毘古）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||`|01| 01=若子宿禰
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree/end}}
『[[日本書紀]]』では父祖に関する明記はないが、孝元天皇7年2月2日条において第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇子の[[彦太忍信命]]（ひこふつおしのまことのみこと）が武内宿禰の祖父とされているため、彦太忍信命が屋主忍男武雄心命の父にあたる。 子に関して、同書[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条では、[[莵道彦]]（[[紀伊国造|紀直]]遠祖）の娘の[[影媛]]（かげひめ）との間に[[武内宿禰]]があるとする{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また[[応神天皇]]9年4月条では、武内宿禰の弟として[[甘美内宿禰]]の名も見える。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、孝元天皇皇子の比古布都押之信命（彦太忍信命）の子が建内宿禰（武内宿禰）になるとしており、少名日子建猪心命は系譜から外れている{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『紀氏系図』では、孝元天皇皇子に彦太忍信命、その子に屋主忍雄命、その子に武内宿禰と甘美内宿禰とする系譜を記載する{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条によると、天皇は[[紀伊国|紀伊]]に行幸して神祇祭祀を行おうとしたが、占いで不吉と出たため、代わりに武雄心命が遣わされた。武雄心命が阿備柏原（あびのかしわばら：現・[[和歌山市]]相坂・松原か）にて留まり住むこと9年、その間に[[影媛]]との間に[[武内宿禰]]を儲けたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[政事要略]]』所引「[[高橋氏文]]」によると、膳氏祖の[[磐鹿六雁命]]が死去した際に、景行天皇によって藤河別命・武男心命（武雄心命）が宣命使として派遣されたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、[[垂仁天皇]]の癸酉年2月1日に武雄心命は住吉大神の願に従って遣わされ、山を寄せて幣とし、阿備柏原社で斎祀し、9年のうちに難破道龍住山（比定地未詳）を申し賜ったという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 紀朝臣 - 石川朝臣同氏。屋主忍雄建猪心命の後。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=屋主忍男武雄心命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[武内宿禰]] * 屋主忍男武雄心命を祭神とする主な神社 **[[江野神社]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]） ** [[若櫻神社]]摂社の高屋安倍神社（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]） **[[武雄神社]]（[[佐賀県武雄市]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:やぬしおしおたけおこころのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 屋主忍男武雄心命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古代]] | 生誕 = | 死没 = | 改名 = | 別名 = 屋主忍男武猪心命、武猪心命、 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = | 父母 = 父：[[彦太忍信命]]、母：不明 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = [[影媛]] | 子 = [[武内宿禰]]、[[甘美内宿禰]] | 特記事項 = }} '''屋主忍男武雄心命'''（やぬしおしおたけおごころのみこと、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。父は[[彦太忍信命]]であるが母に関する記載はない。 『日本書紀』では屋主忍男武雄心命のほか、'''屋主忍男武猪心命'''（やぬしおしおたけいごころのみこと）、'''武猪心命'''、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では'''建猪心命'''、'''屋主忍雄武猪心命'''、『紀氏家牒』逸文では'''屋主忍武雄心命'''、また屋主忍雄命、武男心命等とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』では孝元天皇皇子「少名日子建猪心命」が該当すると見られるが、子孫の記載は無い。 『日本書紀』によって、第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇孫で、[[武内宿禰]]の父とされる人物である{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 系譜 ==

表記は『日本書紀』を第一とし、『古事記』を併記。 {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[孝元天皇]]|02=[[彦太忍信命]]

『[[日本書紀]]』では父祖に関する明記はないが、孝元天皇7年2月2日条において第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇子の[[彦太忍信命]]（ひこふつおしのまことのみこと）が武内宿禰の祖父とされているため、彦太忍信命が屋主忍男武雄心命の父にあたる。 子に関して、同書[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条では、[[莵道彦]]（[[紀伊国造|紀直]]遠祖）の娘の[[影媛]]（かげひめ）との間に[[武内宿禰]]があるとする{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また[[応神天皇]]9年4月条では、武内宿禰の弟として[[甘美内宿禰]]の名も見える。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、孝元天皇皇子の比古布都押之信命（彦太忍信命）の子が建内宿禰（武内宿禰）になるとしており、少名日子建猪心命は系譜から外れている{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『紀氏系図』では、孝元天皇皇子に彦太忍信命、その子に屋主忍雄命、その子に武内宿禰と甘美内宿禰とする系譜を記載する{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条によると、天皇は[[紀伊国|紀伊]]に行幸して神祇祭祀を行おうとしたが、占いで不吉と出たため、代わりに武雄心命が遣わされた。武雄心命が阿備柏原（あびのかしわばら：現・[[和歌山市]]相坂・松原か）にて留まり住むこと9年、その間に[[影媛]]との間に[[武内宿禰]]を儲けたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[政事要略]]』所引「[[高橋氏文]]」によると、膳氏祖の[[磐鹿六雁命]]が死去した際に、景行天皇によって藤河別命・武男心命（武雄心命）が宣命使として派遣されたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、[[垂仁天皇]]の癸酉年2月1日に武雄心命は住吉大神の願に従って遣わされ、山を寄せて幣とし、阿備柏原社で斎祀し、9年のうちに難破道龍住山（比定地未詳）を申し賜ったという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 紀朝臣 - 石川朝臣同氏。屋主忍雄建猪心命の後。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=屋主忍男武雄心命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[武内宿禰]] * 屋主忍男武雄心命を祭神とする主な神社 **[[江野神社]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]） ** [[若櫻神社]]摂社の高屋安倍神社（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]） **[[武雄神社]]（[[佐賀県武雄市]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:やぬしおしおたけおこころのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 屋主忍男武雄心命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古代]] | 生誕 = | 死没 = | 改名 = | 別名 = 屋主忍男武猪心命、武猪心命、 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = | 父母 = 父：[[彦太忍信命]]、母：不明 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = [[影媛]] | 子 = [[武内宿禰]]、[[甘美内宿禰]] | 特記事項 = }} '''屋主忍男武雄心命'''（やぬしおしおたけおごころのみこと、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。父は[[彦太忍信命]]であるが母に関する記載はない。 『日本書紀』では屋主忍男武雄心命のほか、'''屋主忍男武猪心命'''（やぬしおしおたけいごころのみこと）、'''武猪心命'''、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では'''建猪心命'''、'''屋主忍雄武猪心命'''、『紀氏家牒』逸文では'''屋主忍武雄心命'''、また屋主忍雄命、武男心命等とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』では孝元天皇皇子「少名日子建猪心命」が該当すると見られるが、子孫の記載は無い。 『日本書紀』によって、第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇孫で、[[武内宿禰]]の父とされる人物である{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 系譜 ==
'''[[武内宿禰]]関係系図'''

==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]]'''Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

{{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Year of Birth Unknown]] [[Category:Year of Death Unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Year of Birth Unknown]] [[Category:Year of Death Unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of Death Unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - {{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - {{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - {{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi {{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi '''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - '''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] - Kishi Clan - Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}}== See Also == - According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]] [[Category:cer 5]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:cer 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] ]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:cer 5]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}

Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{citation needed|Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}}

revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:屋主忍男武雄心命 up to rev 87525007
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 屋主忍男武雄心命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古代]] | 生誕 = | 死没 = | 改名 = | 別名 = 屋主忍男武猪心命、武猪心命、 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = | 父母 = 父：[[彦太忍信命]]、母：不明 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = [[影媛]] | 子 = [[武内宿禰]]、[[甘美内宿禰]] | 特記事項 = }} '''屋主忍男武雄心命'''（やぬしおしおたけおごころのみこと、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。父は[[彦太忍信命]]であるが母に関する記載はない。 『日本書紀』では屋主忍男武雄心命のほか、'''屋主忍男武猪心命'''（やぬしおしおたけいごころのみこと）、'''武猪心命'''、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では'''建猪心命'''、'''屋主忍雄武猪心命'''、『紀氏家牒』逸文では'''屋主忍武雄心命'''、また屋主忍雄命、武男心命等とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』では孝元天皇皇子「少名日子建猪心命」が該当すると見られるが、子孫の記載は無い。 『日本書紀』によって、第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇孫で、[[武内宿禰]]の父とされる人物である{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 系譜 ==
'''[[武内宿禰]]関係系図'''
表記は『日本書紀』を第一とし、『古事記』を併記。 {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[孝元天皇]]|02=[[彦太忍信命]]
{{Small|（比古布都押之信命）}}|03='''屋主忍男武雄心命'''
{{Small|（古事記なし）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[武内宿禰]]'''
{{Small|（建内宿禰）}}|01=[[羽田矢代|羽田矢代宿禰]]
{{Small|（波多八代宿禰）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[許勢小柄|許勢小柄宿禰]]
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[甘美内宿禰]]
{{Small|（味師内宿禰）}}|01=[[蘇我石川|石川宿禰]]
{{Small|（蘇賀石河宿禰）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01=[[平群木菟|平群木菟宿禰]]
{{Small|（平群都久宿禰）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01=[[紀角|紀角宿禰]]
{{Small|（木角宿禰）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|久米能摩伊刀比売}}
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|怒能伊呂比売}}
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01=[[葛城襲津彦]]
{{Small|（葛城長江曾都毘古）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||`|01| 01=若子宿禰
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree/end}}
『[[日本書紀]]』では父祖に関する明記はないが、孝元天皇7年2月2日条において第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇子の[[彦太忍信命]]（ひこふつおしのまことのみこと）が武内宿禰の祖父とされているため、彦太忍信命が屋主忍男武雄心命の父にあたる。 子に関して、同書[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条では、[[莵道彦]]（[[紀伊国造|紀直]]遠祖）の娘の[[影媛]]（かげひめ）との間に[[武内宿禰]]があるとする{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また[[応神天皇]]9年4月条では、武内宿禰の弟として[[甘美内宿禰]]の名も見える。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、孝元天皇皇子の比古布都押之信命（彦太忍信命）の子が建内宿禰（武内宿禰）になるとしており、少名日子建猪心命は系譜から外れている{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『紀氏系図』では、孝元天皇皇子に彦太忍信命、その子に屋主忍雄命、その子に武内宿禰と甘美内宿禰とする系譜を記載する{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条によると、天皇は[[紀伊国|紀伊]]に行幸して神祇祭祀を行おうとしたが、占いで不吉と出たため、代わりに武雄心命が遣わされた。武雄心命が阿備柏原（あびのかしわばら：現・[[和歌山市]]相坂・松原か）にて留まり住むこと9年、その間に[[影媛]]との間に[[武内宿禰]]を儲けたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[政事要略]]』所引「[[高橋氏文]]」によると、膳氏祖の[[磐鹿六雁命]]が死去した際に、景行天皇によって藤河別命・武男心命（武雄心命）が宣命使として派遣されたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、[[垂仁天皇]]の癸酉年2月1日に武雄心命は住吉大神の願に従って遣わされ、山を寄せて幣とし、阿備柏原社で斎祀し、9年のうちに難破道龍住山（比定地未詳）を申し賜ったという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 紀朝臣 - 石川朝臣同氏。屋主忍雄建猪心命の後。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=屋主忍男武雄心命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[武内宿禰]] * 屋主忍男武雄心命を祭神とする主な神社 **[[江野神社]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]） ** [[若櫻神社]]摂社の高屋安倍神社（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]） **[[武雄神社]]（[[佐賀県武雄市]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:やぬしおしおたけおこころのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|怒能伊呂比売}}
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||)|01| 01=[[葛城襲津彦]]
{{Small|（葛城長江曾都毘古）}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||`|01| 01=若子宿禰
{{Small|（日本書紀なし）}}}} {{familytree/end}}
『[[日本書紀]]』では父祖に関する明記はないが、孝元天皇7年2月2日条において第8代[[孝元天皇]]皇子の[[彦太忍信命]]（ひこふつおしのまことのみこと）が武内宿禰の祖父とされているため、彦太忍信命が屋主忍男武雄心命の父にあたる。 子に関して、同書[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条では、[[莵道彦]]（[[紀伊国造|紀直]]遠祖）の娘の[[影媛]]（かげひめ）との間に[[武内宿禰]]があるとする{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また[[応神天皇]]9年4月条では、武内宿禰の弟として[[甘美内宿禰]]の名も見える。 一方『[[古事記]]』では、孝元天皇皇子の比古布都押之信命（彦太忍信命）の子が建内宿禰（武内宿禰）になるとしており、少名日子建猪心命は系譜から外れている{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 また『紀氏系図』では、孝元天皇皇子に彦太忍信命、その子に屋主忍雄命、その子に武内宿禰と甘美内宿禰とする系譜を記載する{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[景行天皇]]3年2月1日条によると、天皇は[[紀伊国|紀伊]]に行幸して神祇祭祀を行おうとしたが、占いで不吉と出たため、代わりに武雄心命が遣わされた。武雄心命が阿備柏原（あびのかしわばら：現・[[和歌山市]]相坂・松原か）にて留まり住むこと9年、その間に[[影媛]]との間に[[武内宿禰]]を儲けたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[政事要略]]』所引「[[高橋氏文]]」によると、膳氏祖の[[磐鹿六雁命]]が死去した際に、景行天皇によって藤河別命・武男心命（武雄心命）が宣命使として派遣されたという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[住吉大社神代記]]』によれば、[[垂仁天皇]]の癸酉年2月1日に武雄心命は住吉大神の願に従って遣わされ、山を寄せて幣とし、阿備柏原社で斎祀し、9年のうちに難破道龍住山（比定地未詳）を申し賜ったという{{Sfn|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 後裔氏族 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』には、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 右京皇別 紀朝臣 - 石川朝臣同氏。屋主忍雄建猪心命の後。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=屋主忍男武雄心命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|屋主忍男武雄心命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[武内宿禰]] * 屋主忍男武雄心命を祭神とする主な神社 **[[江野神社]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]） ** [[若櫻神社]]摂社の高屋安倍神社（[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]） **[[武雄神社]]（[[佐賀県武雄市]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:やぬしおしおたけおこころのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] {{citation needed|Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto}} {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}}
