{{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]][[Category:Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] }} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]]

These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]]

These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]]

He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[sn:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}}

He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}}

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}}

[[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]][[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]][[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]][[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Kagehime|WD=Q98083008}}|children={{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668}}; {{ill|Umashiuti-no-Sukune|WD=Q18225727}}|father={{ill|Prince Hikofutsuoshinomakoto|WD=Q11487911}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11465637]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Historical Figure | name = Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | alias = Yanushi Oshio Takeigokokoro no Mikoto, Takeigokokoro no Mikoto | burial_place = | titles = | deity_title = | religious_name = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], Mother: Unknown | siblings = | spouse = [[Kagehime]] | children = [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], [[Kanmi no Sukune]] | notable_roles = Served [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] }} '''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto''' (屋主忍男武雄心命, dates unknown) was a figure recorded in ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and other ancient Japanese texts. He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
'''Genealogy of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|-|02|-|03|.| 01={{Supra|8}} [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]|02=[[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]]
{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''
{{Small|(not mentioned in Kojiki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|,|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|-|'|}} {{familytree|border=0|!|}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|)|00|v|01| 00='''[[Takenouchi no Sukune]]'''
{{Small|(Kenuchi no Sukune)}}|01=[[Haneda Yashiro|Haneda Yashiro no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Hada Yashiro no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|!||||)|01| 01=[[Kose no Ogarasu|Kose no Ogarasu no Sukune]]
{{Small|(not mentioned in Nihon Shoki)}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|`|00|)|01| 00=[[Kanmi no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Aji Sukune)}}|01=[[Soga Ishikawa|Ishikawa no Sukune]]
{{Small|(Soga Ishikawa no Sukune)}}}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

{{Small|(Hiko Futsu Oshi no Shin no Mikoto)}}|03='''Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto'''

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto was the son of [[Hikofutsuoshinomakoto no Mikoto]], a prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}. He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}} but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]]

He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:pages]]

He is the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], who became a legendary statesman and hero, and of Kanmi no Sukune, a notable historical figure. == Records == === ''Nihon Shoki'' === The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan) refers to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as an important figure serving during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. In one instance, Emperor Keikō intended to perform a ritual in {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} but found the omens unfavorable. He sent Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto as his proxy to conduct the rituals. Yanushi resided in Abikashihara (modern-day [[:en:Wakayama City|Wakayama City]]) for nine years, during which time he married [[Kagehime]] and fathered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]. === ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' === In the ''Sumiyoshi Taisha Jingūki'' (Chronicles of Sumiyoshi Shrine), Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is described as an emissary to the Sumiyoshi deity. He established a shrine at Abikashihara and consecrated it to the deity after nine years of service. === Clan Genealogies === The genealogies of the [[Kishi Clan]] and related clans trace their ancestry to Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto. These texts identify him as the progenitor of both [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and Kanmi no Sukune. == Descendant Clans == The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' (Newly Compiled Register of Clan Names) identifies the following clans as descendants: - [[Right Capital Imperial Clans]] ** Kishi Clan ** Soga Ishikawa Clan == Worship == Yanushi Oshio Takeokokoro no Mikoto is venerated as a deity in several shrines, including: - [[Eno Shrine]] (Niigata Prefecture) - Takaya Abe Shrine, a sub-shrine of [[Wakasakura Shrine]] (Nara Prefecture) - [[Takeo Shrine]] (Saga Prefecture) == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names|author=Kikawa Gaku|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=2010}} == See Also == - [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|屋主忍男武雄心命|version=87525007|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Yanushi Otake Ogokoro no Mikoto]] [[ja:屋主忍男武雄心命]] [[zh:屋主忍男武雄心命]] {{wikidata link|Q11465637}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

He is noted as the father of [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] and is regarded as an ancestor of various clans. == Genealogy ==
